briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for 2026-06-18.

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Diplomacy and Domestic Implementation Efforts
sections.content: The Republic of Korea government attended the Subsidiary Bodies meeting of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) to discuss implementation strategies for its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) and is actively participating in preparatory meetings for the upcoming COP31 [1, 2, 6, 10]. In these meetings, the importance of clean energy transition was repeatedly emphasized [3, 4], and Climate Response Commissioner Lee Chang-hoon commenced his first multilateral climate diplomacy activities by attending London Climate Action Week [11, 55]. Meanwhile, key issues emerged at the main UN climate conference ahead of COP31 [61], and Jamaica has accepted to host the Ocean Conference in 2029, expanding the scope of international climate cooperation [73].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Domestic Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Various local governments and corporations in Korea are accelerating their efforts towards carbon neutrality and renewable energy transition. Chungju City is actively pursuing the creation of a citizen-led 'Carbon Neutral Green City' [14, 17, 21], and Cheongsong County is encouraging community participation by providing incentives to households excelling in carbon neutrality points [18, 24]. Furthermore, amidst predictions that discussions on integrating public power generation companies will accelerate renewable energy expansion [29, 31], private sector investment and technological development in renewable energy are also active, exemplified by Hana Bank's arrangement of offshore wind project financing and Hyundai Motor Group's utilization of green hydrogen grids [27, 28, 36]. The launch of the 'Energy Demand Statistics Expert Consultative Body' is expected to serve as a crucial compass for achieving carbon neutrality goals [16].

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis Warning Signs and Direct Impacts
sections.content: The global warning signs of the climate crisis are growing louder, and direct impacts are being felt domestically with temperatures exceeding 30 degrees Celsius and heatwave advisories being issued [7, 37, 38]. Internationally, climate change is increasing the influx of migrants [41], causing local extinction of more species in temperate regions [45, 70], and warnings have emerged that it has even reached a critical situation threatening livelihoods in Honduras [51]. In particular, the potential for virus spread due to changes in mosquito habitats [50, 53] and negative impacts on aquaculture and U.S. milk production [48, 57, 66] are amplifying concerns about food security and public health. Research indicating that the top 10% of 'mega-consumers' worldwide cause immense annual climate and biodiversity damage suggests the need for fundamental changes in consumption patterns [75].

sections.title: ⚠️ Social Discourse and Challenges Surrounding Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change is not merely an environmental issue but demands debate and adaptation across various domains including society, politics, and culture. The order to restore climate change-related data deleted during the Trump administration demonstrates shifts in the political landscape [39, 43], and concerns are being raised in some quarters that media interest in climate change is waning [62]. Efforts to respond through education and research are also continuing, such as the introduction of climate change education in the Canadian construction industry [47] and the selection of a climate change impact initiative at Aurora College [60]. However, the discourse surrounding climate change remains complex and multi-layered, encompassing unusual claims that climate change may contribute to an increase in home runs at Dodger Stadium [40], paradoxical views that it has benefited British drinkers [72], and concerns about climate fearmongering [67].

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing. This is a critical time that calls for sustained attention and effort regarding climate change. Thank you.

articles.title: Daytime Highs Exceed 30 Degrees Celsius… Heavy Rain Expected from Night
articles.original_title: 낮 최고 30도 넘어가는 더위··· 밤부터 강한 비
articles.description: A citizen walks through cooling fog installed to alleviate the heat at Seoul Plaza in Jung-gu, Seoul. On Friday, the 19th, daytime high temperatures are expected to exceed 30 degrees Celsius across most parts of the country, requiring preparation for hot weather. Heavy rain is forecast nationwide starting from the night. The Korea Meteorological Administration announced today, 'Heatwave advisories have been issued for parts of the Seoul metropolitan area and inland Gyeongbuk region, where the highest sensible temperatures could exceed 33 degrees Celsius.'

summary.phenomenon: On Friday, the 19th, daytime high temperatures exceeded 30 degrees Celsius across most parts of the country, with heatwave advisories issued for parts of the Seoul metropolitan area and inland Gyeongbuk region, where sensible temperatures reached over 33 degrees Celsius.
summary.outlook: Heavy rain is forecast nationwide starting from the night.

articles.original_description: 한 시민이 서울 중구 서울광장에서 더위를 식히기 위해 설치된 쿨링포그를 맞으며 지나가고 있다. 한수빈 기자금요일인 19일은 전국 대부분 지역의 낮 최고기온이 30도를 넘어 더운 날씨에 대비해야겠다. 밤부터는 전국에 비가 내리겠다.기상청은 이날 “수도권 일부와 경북권 내륙 등지에 폭염특보가 발효돼 최고 체감온도가 33도 이상인 곳도 있겠다”고 예보했다.주요 ···

articles.title: 식량 및 에너지 '메가 소비자', 환경에 연간 5.7조 달러 피해 유발, 연구 결과
articles.original_title: ‘Mega-consumers’ of food and energy cost environment $5.7tn a year, study finds
articles.description: Top 10% generate climate and biodiversity damage bill that exceeds economies of most countries, say researchersThe environmental damage bill racked up by the highest-consuming 10% of the world’s population has reached up to $5.7tn a year – larger than the economy of every country except the US and China, a study has found.Mega-consumers in this group are concentrated in the global north, accounting for more than half the population of the US and 40-45% of people in the EU. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: The top 10% of the global population, referred to as 'mega-consumers,' are causing climate and biodiversity damage amounting to up to $5.7 trillion annually, exceeding the economic size of most countries except the US and China. These consumers are primarily concentrated in the Global North, accounting for over half of the US population and 40-45% of the EU population.
